people.bio: Elizabeth Lynam (b. c. 1830) was a widow and property holder living at Newcastle Court (then known as The Elms) on Newcastle Circus in 1881 and 1891. She was the proprietor of Lynam and Kidd, based at the Castle Works, Castle Road, Nottingham. Lynam and Kidd built the cottages and boundary walls at Papplewick Pumping Station.

occupations.employer: Lynam and Kidd, Castle Works, Castle Road

people.bio: Laura Lawson was a daughter of Elizabeth Lynam, married by 1881.

people.known_as: Lt.-Col. Charles Wilfred Birkin, CMG, JP, DL
people.bio:
Lt.-Col. Charles Wilfred Birkin, CMG, JP, DL (b. 11 February 1865, New Basford, Nottingham; d. 3 April 1932, Lamcote House, Radcliffe-on-Trent) was one of the nine children of Sir Thomas Isaac Birkin (1831–1922), who was the head of T.I. Birkin, one of the largest lace manufacturing businesses in the world. Sir Thomas was created 1st Baronet Birkin of Ruddington Grange on 25 July 1905.

Charles Wilfred was a lace manufacturer and prominent racehorse owner and breeder. During the First World War he served in the 7th Battalion, Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire Regiment. He later served as Deputy Lieutenant of Nottinghamshire (DL) and in 1916 was invested as a Companion, Order of St. Michael and St. George (CMG).

He married Claire Lloyd Howell (b. New York; d. 27 September 1934, West Manor, Ruddington) on 2 July 1892. During the First World War, Claire turned their house into an auxiliary hospital for officers. She was awarded the Medaille de la Reconnaissance Francaise and the Medaille de la Reine Elisabeth of Belgium in recognition of her work.

They had three daughters and one son. Their daughter Winifred May Birkin became famous as Freda Dudley Ward, mistress of the Prince of Wales from 1918 to 1923 and a close confidante of the future King Edward VIII until the beginning of the Wallis Simpson affair in 1934. Their son Sir Charles Lloyd Birkin, 5th Baronet, was a leading British horror writer of the 1960s, writing under the pseudonym Charles Lloyd.

occupations.occupation: Lt.-Colonel, 7th Battalion Nottinghamshire and Derbyshire Regiment

people.bio: Claire Birkin (née Lloyd Howell; b. New York; d. 27 September 1934, West Manor, Ruddington) married Charles Wilfred Birkin on 2 July 1892. During the First World War she converted Newcastle Court into an auxiliary hospital for officers. She was awarded the Medaille de la Reconnaissance Francaise and the Medaille de la Reine Elisabeth of Belgium for her wartime work.

Winifred May Birkin (b. 28 July 1894; d. 16 March 1983) was the eldest daughter of Charles Wilfred and Claire Birkin. She married Rt. Hon. William Dudley Ward PC on 9 July 1913. Ward was Liberal MP for Southampton 1906–22, a leading rower and an Olympic sailor. He was sworn into the Privy Council in 1922, and from that point Winifred was always known as Freda Dudley Ward. They divorced in 1931. She then married Pedro Jose Isidro Manuel Ricardo Mones, Marques de Casa Maury on 20 October 1937. They divorced in 1954. Freda Dudley Ward was most famous for being the mistress of the Prince of Wales from 1918 to 1923 and remaining close to the future King Edward VIII until the beginning of the Wallis Simpson affair in 1934.
Violet Hilda Margaret Birkin (19 March 1899 – 14 June 1953). She married Maj. Douglas Holden Blew-Jones (d. 1947) on 17 December 1918, and later married Geoffrey Hoare in 1949.

Vera Lilian Margaret Birkin (b. 22 April 1903; d. 30 October 1970). She married Maj. Frank James Wriothesley Seely on 5 February 1925. Seely was the grandson of Col. Sir Charles Seely, Baronet, coal owner in Nottinghamshire, Liberal MP for Nottingham 1869–74 and 1880–1885, and Nottingham West 1885–86 and 1892–95. Sir Charles Seely became the first person to be presented with the Freedom of the City of Nottingham in 1895 in recognition of his philanthropic works.

people.known_as: Sir Charles Lloyd Birkin, 5th Baronet Birkin of Ruddington Grange
people.bio: Sir Charles Lloyd Birkin, 5th Baronet Birkin of Ruddington Grange (24 September 1907 – 1985/86) was the son of Charles Wilfred and Claire Birkin. He was educated at Eton College and served as a Captain in the 112th Regiment, 9th Sherwood Foresters during the Second World War. Writing under the pseudonym Charles Lloyd, he was one of the leading British horror writers of the 1960s.

occupations.notes: Pseudonym: Charles Lloyd; one of the leading British horror writers of the 1960s

significant_places.name: Papplewick Pumping Station
significant_places.description: Papplewick Pumping Station is a Victorian water pumping station built 1884–85. Elizabeth Lynam's firm, Lynam and Kidd of Castle Works, Castle Road, built the cottages and boundary walls at the station.

person_connections.connection: Proprietor of Lynam and Kidd, who built the cottages and boundary walls at Papplewick Pumping Station

significant_places.name: Ruddington Grange
significant_places.description: Ruddington Grange was the seat of the Birkin family. Sir Thomas Isaac Birkin was created 1st Baronet Birkin of Ruddington Grange in 1905.

person_connections.connection: Son of Sir Thomas Isaac Birkin, 1st Baronet Birkin of Ruddington Grange
